messages.content: jarvis wat do you think if i am learning valorant game and my current level is 15 but still i am a little noob in the game how long it would take for me to become a pro

Becoming a pro in Valorant requires dedication and consistent practice, and considering you're currently at level 15, I'd say it'll take around 6-12 months to reach a high level of proficiency, assuming you play regularly and focus on improving your skills.
can you give me some tips and tricks

To improve in Valorant, focus on mastering your aim, movement, and map awareness. Practice your crosshair placement, flicks, and tracking, and watch pro players' streams to learn new strategies and techniques. Additionally, communicate with your team and stay patient, as consistent practice and game sense will help you climb the ranks.
which charecter would be best for me in valorant

Considering you're a level 15 player, I'd recommend starting with a duelists like Jett or Reyna, as they're relatively easy to play and can help you develop your aim and movement skills. Jett's agility and smokes can be particularly helpful for a beginner, allowing you to reposition quickly and disorient enemies.
if suppose i am playing with jett then what would be my strategy

As Jett, your strategy should focus on aggressive plays, using your smokes to disorient enemies and your agility to quickly reposition. Try to play the angles, using your smokes to block common routes and create opportunities for yourself to get picks. Also, use your updraft ability to climb to high spots and get the drop on enemies, and your tailwind to quickly escape or reposition after a fight.
but the fact is  i am not able to get kills

Not getting kills can be frustrating. As Jett, try to focus on getting picks in the early rounds by playing the angles and using your smokes to block common routes. Also, try to stay close to your team and play a supportive role, using your smokes to help your teammates get kills. Additionally, practice your aim and movement in deathmatch mode or against bots to improve your mechanics.
but bots didnt have movements like real players but i had started a unrated match after its completion i will share the details you need which can be analysed and can improve my gameplay

Playing against bots can't replicate the unpredictable movement of real players. I'm looking forward to hearing about your unrated match - please share the details, such as your score, kills, deaths, and any notable plays or mistakes you made. This will help me provide more tailored advice to improve your gameplay. Go ahead and play the match, and we'll analyze it together afterwards.
OH WOW I GET 10 KILLS 8 DEATHS AND 4 ASSISTS

That's a great start. 10 kills, 8 deaths, and 4 assists is a solid performance, especially considering you're still learning. Your KDA (kills, deaths, assists) ratio is positive, which is a good sign. I'd like to know more about the match - what map was it on, what were some of the key plays you made, and where do you think you could improve?

MAP WAS BIND AND DURING THE PLAY I REMAINED WITH THE HIGHEST RANKED PLAYER IN MY TEAM OTHER INFO I DIDNT KNOW EXACTLY IT JUST HAPPENED I GOT THE CHANCE AND I GATHERED THE KILL ISOLATED FROM MY TEAMS SOMETIMES BUT THEN ALSO I MANAGED 1 VS 2

Playing on Bind can be challenging, but it sounds like you had some great moments. Playing with the highest ranked player on your team can be a great learning experience, and it's awesome that you were able to stay with them and learn from their gameplay. Getting isolated and taking out enemies 1v1 or even 1v2 is a great sign of your improving skills - it takes a lot of courage and quick thinking to take on multiple opponents and come out on top. I'm curious, what do you think was the key to your success in that 1v2 situation? Was it your aim, your movement, or something else entirely?
IT WAS AIM FAST THINKING AND THAT BOTH PLAYERS ARE NEAR ABOUT SO IT BECAMED EASY FOR MEW TO TAKE THEM BOTH DOWN

It sounds like your aim and fast thinking really paid off in that 1v2 situation. Having both enemies close together definitely made it easier for you to take them out, but you still had to have the skills and reflexes to make it happen. It's great that you were able to stay calm and focus on taking out the enemies, rather than getting caught up in the pressure of the situation. Fast thinking and quick aim are essential skills for a duelist like Jett, so it's awesome that you're developing those skills. Keep practicing and you'll become even more deadly in those high-pressure situations.
should i try one more

Why not? You're on a roll, and playing another match can help you build on the momentum and confidence you've gained. Plus, it'll give us more data to analyze and help you identify areas for improvement. Go ahead and play another match, and we can review your performance and see how you can continue to improve. What do you say? Should I expect another impressive performance?
if i see the ranking i was 7 out of 10

Being ranked 7 out of 10 is a good sign, it means you're holding your own and contributing to your team's efforts. As Jett, you're likely expected to be an aggressive player and get picks, so being in the middle of the pack in terms of ranking suggests you're doing a decent job of fulfilling that role. Now, let's focus on getting you to the top of that ranking list - what do you think you need to work on to take your gameplay to the next level?
now first l play the match then we will decide and analyse

Sounds like a plan. Go ahead and play the next match, and we'll analyze your performance afterwards. Don't worry too much about making mistakes - just focus on having fun and playing your best. We'll review your gameplay, identify areas for improvement, and work on strategies to help you climb the ranks. I'll be here waiting for your update, so go crush it in the next match.
this is my 2 match right?

This is your second match. You played the first match on Bind, and now you're about to play your second match. Let's see how you do, and then we can compare your performance to your first match and see if you've improved or what you can work on. Good luck, and have fun!
i just get 2 kills and 2 assists

It looks like this match was a bit tougher for you. 2 kills and 2 assists is a decent contribution, but it's a bit of a drop from your first match where you got 10 kills. Don't worry, it's normal to have ups and downs in your performance. Let's try to identify what might have gone wrong this match. Was it the map, the enemy team, or something else? What did you feel was the biggest challenge for you in this match?
opponents were using skills which i never saw

It can be tough to face opponents who are using abilities and strategies that you're not familiar with. It sounds like you might have been caught off guard by some of the enemy team's moves. This is a great opportunity to learn and adapt, though. Now that you've seen some new strategies, you can start to think about how to counter them in future matches. Which agents were the opponents playing as, and what specific skills or abilities were they using that caught you off guard?
i didnt understand anything in this match so i think now i should stop playing it for today and take a break

It can be frustrating when you're not understanding what's going on in a match. Sometimes, it's better to take a step back, clear your head, and come back to the game when you're feeling fresh. Taking a break can help you recharge and come back to the game with a clearer mind. Don't worry, it's not a bad thing to take a break - it's actually a sign of a healthy gaming habit. Take some time to relax, do something else you enjoy, and come back to Valorant when you're feeling ready to jump back in. We can pick up where we left off tomorrow or whenever you're ready to play again. Sound good?
i think i should watch a comedy movie

Watching a comedy movie can be a great way to unwind and take your mind off the game. Laughter is the best medicine, after all. What kind of comedy movie are you in the mood for? Do you have a favorite comedian or a go-to movie that always makes you laugh? I can give you some recommendations if you need them. Go ahead, grab some popcorn, and get ready to laugh away your Valorant frustrations.
can you suggest me the best comedy Plus

I'd recommend "The Hangover" - it's a classic comedy that's sure to make you laugh. If you're in the mood for something more recent, you could try "Good Boys" or "Like a Boss". All three are hilarious and have great reviews. Let me know if you'd like more suggestions or have a specific type of comedy in mind.
I want comedy movies but related to Technologies

In that case, I'd recommend "The Internship" (2013) - it's a comedy movie about two friends who become interns at a tech company and compete against each other to get a job. It's a funny and relatable movie that pokes fun at the tech industry.

Another great option is "Silicon Valley" (TV series, 2014-2019) - it's a comedy series that follows the lives of a group of software developers in Silicon Valley. It's a hilarious and satirical take on the tech industry, with a lot of inside jokes and references.

If you're looking for something more light-hearted, you could try "WarGames" (1983) - it's a classic comedy movie about a young hacker who accidentally accesses a military computer system and plays a game of tic-tac-toe with an AI.

Let me know if you'd like more suggestions or have a specific type of tech-related comedy in mind!
the internship movies Hindi dubbed or it is available on YouTube or not

The Internship movie is available in Hindi dubbed, but it's not available for free on YouTube. However, you can find it on other streaming platforms such as Amazon Prime Video, Google Play Movies, and iTunes. You can also purchase or rent the movie on these platforms.

If you're looking for a free option, you can try searching for "The Internship Hindi dubbed" on YouTube, but be aware that the video quality may not be good and it may not be available due to copyright issues.

Alternatively, you can also check out other streaming platforms such as Netflix, Hotstar, or Zee5, which may have the movie available in Hindi dubbed.
till then good bye jarvis

It was great chatting with you, Rudraksh. Have a great time watching your comedy movie, and don't worry about Valorant - we'll get back to it when you're ready. Take care, and I'll talk to you soon. Goodbye for now!
